metadata.title: Diogo Gulhak | Clarity, structure and execution for digital products
metadata.description: Diogo Gulhak's portfolio focused on turning complex context into clearer, more executable and more sustainable products, backed by solid technical foundations and consistent delivery.

header.status: Available to bring more structure, flow and execution clarity to products that need it

hero.eyebrow: Portfolio 2026 · product clarity, technical structure and thoughtful execution
hero.title: I turn complex context into products that are clearer, more executable and easier to evolve.
hero.description: I work best in scenarios where the problem, interface, architecture and delivery all need to become clearer at the same time. Flutter and Angular show up as supporting foundations to make that happen consistently.

highlights.title: 4 published apps
highlights.description: Hands-on experience publishing and evolving mobile products in the Play Store and App Store, with continuity after launch.

highlights.title: Structure for 3 operations
highlights.description: I structured multi-tenant foundations to support different operations without duplication or chaotic maintenance.

highlights.title: Delivery without improvisation
highlights.description: I use CI/CD, modular architecture and release organization to reduce friction across development, QA and publishing.

aside.title: Product reading, technical structure and real execution capacity inside the same line of thought.
aside.description: My best work happens when a solution needs to start well, gain clarity in its flow and keep evolving without turning into operational noise.

details.label: Where I add value
details.value: I organize context, flow and execution so teams can make better decisions and deliver with less friction.

details.label: Technical base
details.value: Flutter, Angular, REST integrations, OAuth/OIDC, FCM and WebView come in as the technical support layer behind stronger product decisions.

details.label: Delivery and evolution
details.value: Published apps, multi-tenant contexts and release pipelines that help the solution keep moving forward without chaos.

items.title: 4 apps in the stores
items.description: Real Play Store and App Store publishing experience with maintenance, fixes and continuous evolution.

items.title: 3 clients on one core
items.description: A white-label, multi-tenant base ready for branding, configuration and growth per operation.

items.title: CI/CD with CodeMagic + GitHub Actions
items.description: Build, versioning and release flows that reduce manual dependency in the delivery cycle.

items.title: Flutter and Angular in real contexts
items.description: Applied experience across both mobile products and multi-tenant SaaS with architecture and UX concerns.

cases.title: Work that shows applied architecture, not decorative storytelling.
cases.description: Each case below was rewritten to make the context, technical decisions, stack and product impact much easier to read.

items.label: Mobile product
items.name: Atlas Mobile Suite
items.headline: A production Flutter application with 4 published apps and a reliable Android and iOS pipeline.
items.context: The challenge was to keep delivery moving without sacrificing the foundation. The work focused on modular architecture, critical integrations and publishing so the product would not depend on manual fixes every time a release was prepared.
highlights: Modular Flutter architecture separating domain, flows and responsibilities by feature.
REST API and authentication integrations with more stable error, session and operational fallback handling.
Android and iOS build and release flow reducing friction between development, QA and store publishing.
items.outcome: A more predictable foundation for product evolution, less friction during releases and better continuity after launch.

actions.label: I need a foundation like this

items.label: White-label
items.name: White-label Platform
items.headline: One app core, 3 clients and branding rules without turning maintenance into chaos.
items.context: The challenge was to support different operations with their own identity, configuration and evolution path while avoiding forks and repeated implementation for each client request.
highlights: Clear separation between the shared core and client-specific customizations to preserve product velocity.
Extension points for theme, navigation, rules and features without breaking the common base.
Cleaner maintenance, smoother technical onboarding and more controlled delivery per client.
items.outcome: Less duplication, more consistency across clients and a base ready to grow with control.

items.label: SaaS frontend
items.name: Angular Multi-tenant SaaS
items.headline: Angular frontend for SaaS where onboarding, authentication, navigation, i18n and design tokens work as a system.
items.context: In multi-tenant SaaS, the experience breaks quickly when onboarding, authentication and navigation are treated as isolated flows. The job was to structure the interface as a platform rather than a loose set of screens.
highlights: Application structure supporting authentication, access states and navigation with a more consistent reading model.
Internationalization and design tokens sustaining UI evolution without losing coherence.
Frontend thinking oriented toward product, connecting UX, componentization and maintenance.
items.outcome: Stronger interface consistency, clearer onboarding and a frontend foundation ready to scale features without visual or technical noise.

actions.label: Talk about a SaaS like this

approach.title: Product decisions, architecture and delivery inside the same line of reasoning.
approach.description: My best work happens when the interface needs to mature, the codebase must stay readable and the release flow cannot rely on improvisation.

principles.title: Architecture that reduces future noise
principles.description: I structure apps and frontends so new features can enter with less coupling, less rework and more clarity for the team.

principles.title: Interface with product intent
principles.description: Onboarding, authentication, navigation, i18n and design tokens are not visual details. They are part of product coherence.

principles.title: Delivery with operational support
principles.description: CodeMagic, GitHub Actions, store publishing and release standards come in early to prevent bottlenecks when the product accelerates.

approach.experienceTitle: Applied experience in mobile products and SaaS with direct operational impact.

experienceItems.summary: Modular architecture, REST integrations, OAuth/OIDC, FCM and WebView, with continuous delivery to the Play Store and App Store.
Experience with onboarding, authentication, navigation, internationalization and design tokens for products with multiple usage contexts.
Shared foundations, client-specific configuration and release pipelines built to reduce rework and increase delivery predictability.

finalCta.title: If you need architecture, interface and release to become clearer, I can help move that forward.
finalCta.description: I can contribute to Flutter apps, Angular fronts and multi-tenant foundations that need structure, delivery predictability and more mature technical communication.
